content:
INT. VIOLET'S BEDROOM/UPSTAIRS HALLWAY - TWILIGHT
 8
 She gathers herself to stand. Moves to the door. We FOLLOW HER CLOSELY. Her hair unkempt, her steps unsteady, into --
The reasons why we partake are anymore inconsequential.
The hallway, walls lined with photos of long-dead pioneer ancestors and faded school photographs of three daughters.

The facts are: my wife takes pills and I drink. That's the bargain we've struck, just one paragraph of our marriage contract... cruel covenant. And these facts have over time made burdensome the maintenance of traditional American routine.
She makes her way to the stairs starts down.
Rather than once more vow abstinence with my fingers crossed in the queasy hope of righting our ship, I've chosen to turn my life over to a Higher Power and join the ranks of the Hiring Class.
The light from the study slices across the living room.
It's not a decision with which I'm entirely comfortable. I know how to launder my dirty undies. Done it all my life, but I'm finding it's getting in the way of my drinking.

She can see a portion of Beverly's desk, a woman's legs. "August" 9/21/12 FINAL WHITE Draft
 4. 8
 CONTINUED:
 8

Sorry about the heat in here. My wife is cold-blooded and not just in the metaphorical sense. She does not believe in air-conditioning... as if it is a thing to be disbelieved. I knew your father, you know. Bought many a watermelon from Mr Youngblood's fruit stand. He did pass, didn't he??
Yes, sir.
May I ask how?
He had a heart attack. Fell into a flatbed truck full of wine grapes.

Wine grapes. In Oklahoma.
 I'm sorry.
Yes?

Did you pullish? Did you...Oh, goddamn
 * it... did. You. Are the police here?
No...
She stands in the shadows of the living room, confused.
Am I looking through window? A window?
Can you come here?
She steps into the study, emerging from the darkness into light to reveal: VIOLET WESTON. Dissipated, dishevelled, late sixties. She wears pajamas and a much slept-in robe.

Oh.
 Hello.

She's staring at a woman sitting in front of Beverly's desk: JOHNNA. Thirty, Native American, simply dressed. "August" 9/21/12 FINAL WHITE Draft
 5. 8
 CONTINUED:
 8
Johnna, the young woman I told you about.
You tell me she's a woman. Wo-man. Whoa-man.
That I'm hiring --
Oh, you hire women's now the thing. I thought you meant the other woman.
To cook and clean, take you to the clinic and to the --
over-articulating) In the int'rest of ...civil action, your par-tic-u-lars way of speak- king, I thought you meant you had thought a whoa-man to be HIRED!
I don't understand you.
winsome, to Johnna) Hello.
Hello.
I'm sorry. (curtsies) Like this.
Yes, ma'am.
You're very pretty.
Thank you.

Are you an Indian? "August" 9/21/12 FINAL WHITE Draft
 6. 8
 CONTINUED:
 8
Yes, ma'am.

VIOLET
 * What kind?
 *
 JOHNNA
 * Cheyenne.
 *
Do you think I'm pretty?
Yes, ma'am.
curtsies again) Like...this? (curtsies again) Like this?
She stumbles, catches herself.
Careful...
You're the house now. I'm sorry, I took some medicine for my mussss... muscular.
Why don't you go back to bed, sweetheart?
Why don't you go fuck a fucking sow's ass?
All right.
I'm sorry. I'll be sickly sweet. I'm soooooooo sweet. In-el-abrially sweet.

INT. THE WESTON HOUSE - DAY
 14
 Ivy steps into the dark house.
 Drapes drawn, lights off.
Mom...? (no answer) Mom?

Steps into the open door of her father's study. His vacant desk chair, untouched papers, dust mots settling in the sunlight. She takes a moment, then heads upstairs. CARRYING US with her. Finds Violet, in her bedroom, sitting in front of her vanity in near darkness, smoking and on the phone.
You've been out there...?
Barely acknowledges Ivy's arrival. The room is unruly. Bed unmade. Clothes draped over chairs. Dresser and night-stands cluttered with pills, tissue boxes, creams and lotions.

...You're going out yourself...?
 *
Ivy wanders into the bath. More pills, wet towels on the floor. She turns off the dripping faucet. Picks up towels.
Stop that...
Violet is off the phone, standing in hall, watching Ivy. Ivy stops, briefly chastened. Violet opens a bottle of pills.
You call Barb? What'd she say?
She's on her way.
What'd you tell her?

I told her Dad was missing. "August" 9/21/12 FINAL WHITE Draft
 11. 14
 CONTINUED:
 14
Did you tell her how long he'd been missing?
Five days.
What did she say?
She said she was on her way.
Goddamn it, Ivy, what did she say?
She said she was on her way.

You're hopeless. (heads back into her room) Goddamn your father for putting me through this. Seen that office of his, all that mess? I can't make heads or tails of it. He hired this Indian for some goddamn reason and now I have a stranger in my house. What's her name?
Ivy follows her mother, returns to tidying up.

Johnna.
 Who was on the phone?
This house is falling apart, something about the basement or the sump pump or the foundation. I don't know anything about it. I can't do this by myself.
I called Karen.
What did she say?
She said she'd try to get here.

She'll be a big fat help, just like you. (MORE) "August" 9/21/12 FINAL WHITE Draft
 12. 14
 CONTINUED:
 14 VIOLET (takes another pill) I need Barb.
What's Barb going to be able to do?
Ivy moves on to hanging clothes back in the jammed closet.
What did you do to your hair?
I had it straightened.

You had it straightened.
 Why would anybody do that?
I just wanted a change.
You're the prettiest of my three girls, but you always look like a schlub. Why don't you wear makeup?
Do I need makeup?
All women need makeup. Don't let anybody tell you different. The only woman who was pretty enough to go without makeup was Elizabeth Taylor and she wore a ton. Stand up straight.
Mom.
Your shoulders are slumped and your hair's all straight and you don't wear makeup. You look like a lesbian.
Violet takes another pill.
You could get a decent man if you spruced up. A bit, that's all I'm saying.

I'm not looking for a man. "August" 9/21/12 FINAL WHITE Draft
 13. 14
 CONTINUED:
 14
There are a lot of losers out there, don't think I don't know that. But just because you got a bad one last time doesn't mean --
Barry wasn't a loser.
Barry was an asshole. I warned you from the jump, first time you brought him over here in his little electric car with his stupid orange hair and that turban --
It wasn't a turban --
You work at a college. Don't tell me there aren't people coming through the door of that library every day.
You want me to marry some eighteen year old boy from one of these hick towns?
They still have teachers at TU, right? They did when your father taught there.
Violet takes another pill.
How many was that?
I wasn't counting.
Violet takes another pill.
Is your mouth burning?

Like a son-of-a-bitch.
 My tongue is on fire.

Are you supposed to be smoking? "August" 9/21/12 FINAL WHITE Draft
 14. 14
 CONTINUED:
 14
Is anybody supposed to smoke?
You have cancer of the mouth.
Just leave it alone.

IVY
 * (after a moment)
 * Are you scared?
Course I'm scared. And you are a comfort, sweetheart. Thank God one of my girls stayed close to home.
Outside, the sound of a CAR pulling up. Ivy pulls back the drape and the shade, finds a big Cadillac arriving.
Aunt Mattie Fae's here.
She means to come in here and tell me what's what.
I don't know how Uncle Charlie puts up with it.
He smokes a lot of grass.
He does?
He smokes a lot of grass.

INT. VIOLET'S BEDROOM - DAY
 17
 Violet hangs up the phone. Sits for a long moment, absorbing what she's heard. Mattie Fae watches from her spot sitting
 * on the corner of the bed, concerned. Ivy is in the door.
 *
They checked the hospitals, no Beverly.

Who's this now? The highway patrol?
 *
No, the sheriff, the Gilbeau boy.
What else did he say?
The boat's missing.
Dad's boat?
I asked the sheriff to send a deputy out to the dock to check if anybody had seen him and his boat is gone.

INT. WESTON HOUSE - AFTERNOON
 20
 Charlie is poking around the old stereo, finds an LP, the TV beside him is tuned to a Royals game.
Violet's a Clapton fan?
Johnna passes through, Charlie holds up his empty bottle.
`Scuse me, dear...could I trouble you for another beer?

Goddam it, she's not a waitress. "August" 9/21/12 FINAL WHITE Draft
 22. 20
 CONTINUED:
 20
I know that.
Then get your own beer.
takes the empty/goes) I'll get it.
I don't believe you. Watchin' a ball game, drinkin' beers. You have any sense of what's going on around you?
Am I supposed to sit here like a statue? You're drinking whiskey.
I'm having a cocktail.
You're drinking straight whiskey!
Just... show a little class.
Mom?
Barbara and Bill have entered, are quickly descended upon by Mattie Fae and Charlie. Hugs, overlapping dialogue.

BARBARA Oh my God, Barbara --! You
 Hi, Aunt Mattie Fae -- give me some sugar!
 BILL Bill!
 Look how skinny you
 Hi, Mattie Fae. are!
Hi, Charlie.
Jean enters behind her parents, stands sheepishly.

Oh my gosh, will you look at this one? Come here and give your Aunt Mattie Fae some sugar! "August" 9/21/12 FINAL WHITE Draft
 23. 20
 CONTINUED:
 20
 CHARLIE My gosh, you're so big! Look
 `Lo, Bill. Man you have at your boobs! Last time I
 dropped some weight, haven't saw you, you looked like a
 you? Hello, sweetheart. little boy!
Hi, Uncle Charlie.
How was the flight from Denver?
Fine...
Violet appears on the stairs, rushes to Barbara.
Barb...
It's okay, Mom. I'm here, I'm here. Shh, it's okay, I'm here.
Ivy appears at the top of the stairs, watches her mother in her sister's arms. Bill turns to Charlie, quietly:
No word then?

CHARLIE
 MATTIE FAE No.
 No, huh-uh.
 VIOLET
 BARBARA What am I going to do?
 It's okay, Mom.
Did you see Bill and Jean?
Violet takes them in, disoriented.

Yes.
 Hi, Bill.
Hello, Violet.
I'm just so scared.

Of course you are, poor thing. "August" 9/21/12 FINAL WHITE Draft
 24. 20
 CONTINUED:
 20
sees Jean) Well, look at you.
Isn't she the limit? Look at her boobs!
O-kay, we've all stared at my tits now.
They're just so darn big.
Vi hugs Jean. Johnna slips in, leaves a beer for Charlie.
You're just the prettiest thing. Thank you for coming to see me.
Ivy, I didn't see you up there.
Ivy, still standing above on the stairs.
It looked crowded.

God, you look good.
 Doesn't she look good, Bill?

BILL
 BARBARA Yes, she does.
 I love your hair, that looks great.
She had it straightened. Barbara, or Bill, it doesn't matter, I need you to go through Beverly's things, help me with this paperwork.
Well... we can do that, Mom.
I was going to help with --

No, now that desk of his is such a mess and I get confused -- "August" 9/21/12 FINAL WHITE Draft
 25. 20
 CONTINUED:
 20
I'll take care of it, Violet --
to Charlie) Which room are you in?
We're gonna head home soon.
You're going back to Tulsa?
We have to, we left in such a rush we didn't get anyone to take care of the damn dogs. Anyway, I know you want to spend some time with these girls.
How about Little Charles, can't he take care of the dogs?
Well, yeah, I guess he could --

MATTIE FAE
 CHARLIE No, he can't. We have to get
 Maybe we should call him, back.
 Mattie Fae --
We talked about this.
Mom, can Jean stay in the attic?
No, that's where what's-her-name lives.
Johnna.
Who's Johnna?
She's the Indian who lives in my attic.

INT. THE BACK PORCH - LATE AFTERNOON
 22
 A screened in back porch off the kitchen. Bill and Barb sit at an old linoleum table.
This was when?
Violet stands smoking, unhappily watching Charlie and Mattie Fae climb into the Caddie and disappear down the gravel drive, heading back to Tulsa.
Saturday morning. The Indian girl made us biscuits and gravy. We ate some, he walked out the door, this door right there. Got into his truck. And that was it.
Johnna enters with her mint, crosses into the kitchen.
He just left...?
I went to bed Saturday night, got up Sunday... still no Beverly. I didn't make much of it, thought he'd gone out on a bender.
Why would he do that? Not like he couldn't drink at home. Unless you were riding his ass.

I never said anything to him about his drinking, never got on him about it. "August" 9/21/12 FINAL WHITE Draft
 28. 22
 CONTINUED:
 22
Really.
VILOET Barbara, I swear. He could drink himself into obliv-uh, obliv-en-um...
Oblivion.
So Sunday, still no sign of him...

Yes, Sunday. No sign. I started getting worried, don'tcha know. That's when I got worked up about that safety deposit box.
 We kept an awful lot of cash in that box, some expensive jewelry. I had a diamond ring in that box appraised at seven thousand dollars --
Johnna returns with glasses of iced tea, each with a sprig of mint, delivers them to Bill and Barbara.
Wait, wait, wait, I'm missing something. Why do you care about a safety deposit box?
Well, I know what you'll say about this, but, your father and I had an urge-ment... arrangement. If something were to ever happen to one of us, the other one would go empty that box.
Because...
The money and jewelry gets rolled into the estate, bank seals the box until probate is settled. Can take months.
Right, that's right --

You're such a fucking cynic. "August" 9/21/12 FINAL WHITE Draft
 29. 22
 CONTINUED:
 22
I knew you would disapprove --
Johnna cuts into a freshly baked apple pie in the kitchen.
Okay, what about the safety deposit box?
I had to wait for the bank to open on Monday. And after I emptied that box, I called the police and reported him missing. Monday morning.
And you only had Ivy call me today?
I didn't want to worry you, honey --

BARBARA
 BILL Jesus Christ.
 Vi, you sure there wasn't some event that triggered his leaving, some incident.
You mean like a fight.
Johnna places pieces of pie in front of Bill and Barb.
Yes.
No. And we fought enough... you know... but no, he just left.
Maybe he needed some time away from you.
That's nice of you to say.
Good old unfathomable Dad.

Oh. That man. What I first fell of with -- fell in love with, you know, was his mystery. I thought it was sexy as hell. (MORE) "August" 9/21/12 FINAL WHITE Draft
 30. 22
 CONTINUED:
 22 VIOLET You knew he was the smartest one in the room, knew if he just said something... knock you out. But he'd just stand there, little smile on his face... not say a word. Sexy.

INSIDE THE KITCHEN
 23
 Ivy enters with her coffee cup, runs water in it at the sink. Outside, her mother, sister, and Bill on the back porch.
You can't think of anything unusual --
Johnna sits at the kitchen table behind Ivy. Johnna stands, joins Ivy at the sink. Ivy hadn't seen her there.
He hired this woman. He didn't ask me, just hired this woman to come live in our house. Few days before he left.
You don't want her here.
She's a stranger in my house. There's an Indian in my house.
Ivy looks to Johnna, embarrassed. But Johnna just takes Ivy's cup from her, finishes cleaning it.
You have a problem with Indians, Violet?
I don't know what to say to an Indian.
They're called Native Americans now, Mom.
Who makes that decision?

It's what they like to be called. "August" 9/21/12 FINAL WHITE Draft
 31. 23
 CONTINUED:
 23
They aren't any more native than me.
In fact, they are.
What's wrong with Indian?
Why's it so hard to call people --
Let's just call the dinosaurs "Native Americans" while we're at it.
She may be an Indian, but she makes the best goddamn apple pie I ever ate.

Johnna smiles, nods to Ivy.
 Leaves the kitchen.
He hired a cook. It doesn't make any sense. We don't eat.
And now you get biscuits and gravy. Kind of nice, huh?
Nice for you, now. But you'll be gone soon enough, never to return.
a warning) Mom...
When was the last time you were here?

BARBARA
 VIOLET Don't get started on that --
 Really, I don't even remember.
I'm very dutiful, Mom, I call, I write, I send presents --

You do not write -- "August" 9/21/12 FINAL WHITE Draft
 32. 23
 CONTINUED:
 23
Presents on birthdays, Mother's Day --
Ivy eavesdrops at the sink, unsure if she should stay or go.
Because you're "dutiful."
All right, now --
I don't care about you two. I'd like to see my granddaughter every now --
Well, you're seeing her now.
But your father. You broke his heart when you moved away.
That is wildly unfair.

Bill stands, picks up a plate, pushes his way back into the kitchen. Ivy hears him coming, but doesn't have time to escape. Goes to the refrigerator instead.
 BILL
 VIOLET Am I going to have to
 You know you were Beverly's separate you two?
 favorite; don't pretend you don't know that.
 Barbara follows Bill. Ivy finds iced tea, pours herself some. Tries to make herself invisible -- it's not hard to do.
I'd prefer to think my parents loved all their children equally.
Violet trails behind them into the kitchen.

I'm sure you'd prefer to think that Santy Claus brought you presents at Christmas, too. If you'd had more than one child, you'd know a parent always has favorites. Mattie Fae was my mother's favorite. Big deal. I got used to it. You were your Daddy's favorite. "August" 9/21/12 FINAL WHITE Draft
 33. 23
 CONTINUED:
 23
Barbara notices Ivy, standing there, Christ. This isn't a conversation she'd like to be having in front of her sister. Violet sees Ivy too -- could care less.
Broke his heart.
What was I supposed to do?! Colorado gave Bill twice the money he was making at TU --
Why are we even getting into this?
You think Daddy wouldn't have jumped at the chance Bill got?
You're wrong there. You never would've gotten Beverly Weston out of Oklahoma.
Daddy gave me his blessing.
S what he told you.
Now you're going to tell me the true story, some terrible shit Daddy said behind my back?
Hey, enough. Everybody's on edge --
Beverly didn't say terrible things behind your back --
Vi, come on --

He just told me he's disappointed in you because you settled. He thought
 * you had talent, as a writer.
 *

Daddy never said anything like that to you. What a load of absolute horseshit. "August" 9/21/12 FINAL WHITE Draft
 34. 23
 CONTINUED:
 23
Oh, horseshit, horseshit, let's all say horseshit. Say horseshit, Bill.

INT. BATHROOM - LATE AFTERNOON
 24
 Violet is closing the bathroom door.
 Barb stops her.
Are you high?
Excuse me.
I mean literally. You taking something?
A muscle relaxer.
Listen to me: I will not go through this with you again.
I don't know what you're talking about.

These fucking pills? Calls at three
 * AM about people in your backyard?
Stop yelling at me!
The police, all the rest of it?
It's not the same thing. I didn't have a reason.

So now it's okay to get hooked because you have a reason. "August" 9/21/12 FINAL WHITE Draft
 35. 24
 CONTINUED:
 24
I'm not hooked on anything.
I don't want to know if you are or not, I'm just saying I won't go --

I'm not.
 I'm in pain.
Because of your mouth.
Yes, because my mouth burns from the chemotheeeahh --
Are you in a lot of pain?
Violet starts to break down, sits on the lidded toilet.
Yes, I'm in pain. I have got... gotten cancer. In my mouth. And it burns like a... bullshit. And Beverly's disappeared and you're yelling at me.
I'm not yelling at you.
You couldn't come home when I got cancer but as soon as Beverly disappeared you rushed back --

INT. UPSTAIRS HALLWAY - MOMENTS LATER
 36
 Barbara, bleary-eyed, moves quickly down the dark hall in her robe. Bill follows in his boxers and T-shirt, pulling on pants. Barb goes to Vi's door, KNOCKS.
Mom?
She opens the door. Over her we FIND: Violet, entombed in her room. Squinting against the intrusive hall light.
Mom, wake up, the sheriff's here.

Did you call them?
 I dig in call them.

Mom.
 The sheriff is here.
Inna esther?
What?
Inna esther broke. `N pays me `em...sturck...struck.

INT. LIVING ROOM - PRE-DAWN
 40
 The music is LOUD. We follow Barbara and Jean halfway down the stairs to REVEAL: Violet, high as a kite, doing a jerky little dance by the stereo. The Sheriff stands uncomfortably by the door, his hat in hand. She shuffles over to him.

Izza story. Barely's back.
 Did sum Beer-ley come home?
SHERIFF Ma'am?

Gizza cig... some cigezze?
 Cig-zezz, cig-zizz... cig-uhzzz...
She laughs at her inability to speak. He takes a Pall Mall from his shirt pocket, hands it to her. Lights it for her.
In the archa, archa-tex? I'm in the bottom. Inna bottom of them. (and) Mm, good beat, right?

He nods.
 Bill comes back in from outside.
Barbara?! Is Barbara here?!
quietly) Right here, Mom...
Johnna steps in from the kitchen, pensively observing.

Mm, good beat, right?
 Idn't it's a good beat? (MORE) "August" 9/21/12 FINAL WHITE Draft
 47. 40
 CONTINUED:
 40 VIOLET Mmmm, I been on the music... pell man onna sheriff. Armen in tandel s'lossle, s'lost? Lost?! From the day, the days. Am Beerly... and Beverly lost?
Violet abandons her dance, separates invisible threads in the air. The others stand frozen, staring at her.
And then you're here. And Barbara, and then you're here, and Beverly, and then you're here, and then you're here, and then you're here, and then you're here, and then you're here...

INT. VIOLET'S BEDROOM - DAY
 51
 Violet pulls a dress from the closet; Mattie Fae sits, rooting through a box of photos; Ivy stands by the door.

It won't kill you to try it on -- "August" 9/21/12 FINAL WHITE Draft
 53. 51
 CONTINUED:
 51
Oh, this is a sweet one, Vi --
I find this a tidge morbid, frankly --

MATTIE FAE
 VIOLET Look at this, Ivy --
 What's morbid about it?
It's not my style, Mom.
You don't have a style, that's the point.
Where was this taken?

New York.
 On the first book tour.
I don't have your style, I have a style of my own.
You wore a suit to your father's funeral. A woman doesn't wear a suit to a funeral.
God, you're weird; it's a black suit.
You look like a magician's assistant.
Little Charles has been talking about moving to New York. Can you picture that?
Don't discourage him now --

He wouldn't last a day in that city. They'd tear him apart.
 IVY I could kill that kid --
 Why do you feel it necessary to insult me? "August" 9/21/12 FINAL WHITE Draft
 54. 51
 CONTINUED:
 51
Stop being so sensitive.
He overslept? For his Uncle's funeral? A noon service?
I'm sure there's more to the story --
Don't make excuses for him. That's what Charlie does. Thirty-seven years old and can't drive? Who can't drive?
Violet pulls more and more clothes from the closet, dumping them on the bed. The pile is getting very large.
I've seen a chimp drive.
Why are you giving away your clothes?

All this shit's going. I don't plan to spend the rest of my days looking at what used to be. I want that shit in the office gone, I want these clothes I'm never going to wear gone. I mean look at these fucking shoes -- (holds up spiked heels) Even if I didn't fall on my face, can you imagine anything less attractive, my swollen ankles and varicose veins? And my toenails, good God: anymore they could dig through cement.

INT. VIOLET'S BEDROOM - DAY
 54
 The closet is mostly empty now, the bed overflowing with discarded clothes. Mattie Fae nurses a cocktail, hands a
 * photo to Violet.
Look at me. (shows photo to Ivy) Look at me.
You're beautiful, Mom.
I was beautiful. Not anymore.

MATTIE FAE
 IVY Oh, now --
 You're still beautiful.

One of those lies we tell to give us comfort. Women are beautiful when they're young and not after. Men can still preserve their sex appeal into old age. Not those men like you see with shorts and those little purses around their waists. Some men can maintain a weary masculinity. Women just get old and fat and wrinkly.

I beg your pardon? "August" 9/21/12 FINAL WHITE Draft
 59. 54
 CONTINUED:
 54
Think about the last time you went to the mall and saw some sweet little gal and thought she's a cute trick. What makes her that way? Taut skin, firm boobs, an ass above her knees.
I'm still very sexy, thank you very much.

You're about as sexy as a wet card- board box, Mattie Fae, you and me both. Look, wouldn't we be better off if we stopped lying about these things and told the truth? "Women aren't sexy when they're old." I can live with that. Can you live with that?
What about Sophia Loren? What about Lena Horne? She stayed sexy till she was eighty.
Violet finds something else in the closet for Ivy to try.
The world is round. Get over it. Now try this dress on.
I'm sorry, I won't.
You don't know how to attract a man. I do. That's something I always --
We just buried my father, I'm not trying to attract --!
I'm not talking about today, dummy, this is something you can wear --

I have a man. All right?
 I have a man.

You said you weren't looking for a man -- "August" 9/21/12 FINAL WHITE Draft
 60. 54
 CONTINUED:
 54
And I'm not. Because I have one. Okay? Now will you leave it alone?

VIOLET
 MATTIE FAE No, I won't leave it alone.
 No, let's not leave it alone.
I wish you both could see the brainsick looks on your faces --
Who is it?

Nobody.
 Forget it --
Tell us, is he someone from school? How old is he, what's he do --?
I'm not telling you anything so --
You have to tell us something!
No, I really don't.
Are you in love, Ivy?

INT. BEVERLY'S STUDY - AFTERNOON
 61
 Violet stands in the middle of the room. Sunlight streams in through the windows surrounding her. Approaches Beverly's swivel chair, touches the back, ...slowly spins it ...sits.

August... your month. Locusts are raging, "Summer psalm become summer wrath." `Course it's only August out there. In here... who knows? (and then) All right... okay. "The Carriage held but just Ourselves," dum-de-dum...mm, best I got... Emily Dickenson's all I got... something something, "Horse's Heads Were Toward Eternity..."
Produces a bottle of pills, shakes one out, takes it.
That's for me. One for me.

Surveys the photos behind his desk. The girls. Vi and Bev together in happier times. Picks up the hardback Meadowlark Bill left. Finds Beverly's reading glasses on the desk, puts them on. Thumbs through it, finds the dedication: simply:
For My Violet...
Violet smiles ruefully, takes another pill.
For the girls, God love `em.

INT. DINING ROOM - AFTERNOON
 64
 Barbara, Bill, Mattie Fae, Charlie, Karen, and Steve are already seated. The men have removed their suit coats.
Pass the casserole, please?
My casserole's coming.
I'll eat some of yours, too --
calling out) Mom?! Let's eat!
Little Charles and Ivy enter with the casserole.
There he is. I wanted to put you at a kid's table but they wouldn't let me.

Where do you want this? "August" 9/21/12 FINAL WHITE Draft
 72. 64
 CONTINUED:
 64

Ad-lib greetings, hugs, handshakes, Karen's introduction of Steve. Ivy slips in and takes her seat. Little Charles goes to put Mattie Fae's casserole on the table, but drops it.
 It lands on the floor with a sickening SPLAT.
